classes.name: Announcement
classes.definition: A public consortium announcement or news post.

classes.name: BehavioralSensor
classes.definition: Devices capturing behavior â€” video, pose, gaze, movement.

properties.name: frameRate
properties.definition: Frame rate in fps.

classes.name: CreativeWork
classes.definition: The most generic kind of creative work, including books, articles, software, datasets.

classes.name: Dandiset
classes.definition: A DANDI-archived dataset released by BBQS.

classes.name: EnvironmentalSensor
classes.definition: Devices sensing the environment or spatial context around a subject (LiDAR, mmWave, GPS, RFID, flow, ultrasonic mic).

classes.name: Event
classes.definition: An event happening at a certain time and location.

classes.name: FundedProject
classes.definition: A BBQS research project â€” the scientific work tied to one or more NIH awards.

properties.name: instrument
properties.definition: Devices used in the project.

classes.name: FundingAgency
classes.definition: An organization that funds BBQS research (typically NIH).

classes.name: Grant
classes.definition: A grant, typically financial or otherwise quantifiable, of resources.

classes.name: ImagingSystem
classes.definition: Large-scale imaging systems (fMRI, two-photon, thermal imaging).

properties.name: modality
properties.definition: Imaging modality â€” fMRI, two-photon, thermal.

classes.name: JobPosting
classes.definition: A listing that describes a job opening in a certain organization.

classes.name: NIHGrant
classes.definition: An NIH award funding BBQS work. Encodes the funding mechanism (R61, R34, RF1, etc.).

properties.name: mechanism
properties.definition: NIH funding mechanism â€” R61, R34, RF1, U01, â€¦

classes.name: NeuralRecordingDevice
classes.definition: Devices that record neural activity directly (Neuropixels, wireless neural, iEEG, EEG, OPM).

properties.name: channelCount
properties.definition: Number of recording channels.

properties.name: samplingRate
properties.definition: Sampling rate in Hz.

classes.name: Organization
classes.definition: An organization such as a school, NGO, corporation, club, or funding body.

classes.name: Person
classes.definition: A person (alive, dead, undead, or fictional).

properties.name: email
properties.definition: Primary and secondary emails. PII â€” restricted in the public API.

properties.name: role
properties.definition: BBQS role â€” PI, Co-I, Trainee, Collaborator.

classes.name: PhysiologicalSensor
classes.definition: Devices measuring peripheral physiology (EDA, ECG, EMG, PPG, respiration, cortisol, skin temp).

properties.name: samplingRate
properties.definition: Sampling rate in Hz.

classes.name: Product
classes.definition: Any offered product or service â€” here specialized as scientific instruments.

properties.name: category
properties.definition: BBQS canonical device category (one of the 32).

classes.name: Project
classes.definition: An enterprise planned to achieve a particular aim.

classes.name: Publication
classes.definition: A peer-reviewed publication produced under BBQS support.

classes.name: ResearchOrganization
classes.definition: A university, institute, or lab that participates in BBQS.

classes.name: SoftwareTool
classes.definition: A software package, pipeline, or analysis library released by BBQS.

classes.name: Taxon
classes.definition: A set of organisms asserted to represent a natural cohesive biological unit.

classes.name: Thing
classes.definition: The most generic type on schema.org.

classes.name: AnatomicalStructure
classes.definition: Any part of the human body, typically a component of an anatomical system. Organs, tissues, and cells are all anatomical structures.

properties.name: associatedPathophysiology
properties.definition: If applicable, a description of the pathophysiology associated with the anatomical system, including potential abnormal changes in the mechanical, physical, and biochemical functions of the system.

properties.name: bodyLocation
properties.definition: Location in the body of the anatomical structure.

properties.name: connectedTo
properties.definition: Other anatomical structures to which this structure is connected.

properties.name: diagram
properties.definition: An image containing a diagram that illustrates the structure and/or its component substructures and/or connections with other structures.

properties.name: partOfSystem
properties.definition: The anatomical or organ system that this structure is part of.

properties.name: relatedCondition
properties.definition: A medical condition associated with this anatomy.

properties.name: relatedTherapy
properties.definition: A medical therapy related to this anatomy.

properties.name: subStructure
properties.definition: Component (sub-)structure(s) that comprise this anatomical structure.

classes.name: AnatomicalSystem
classes.definition: An anatomical system is a group of anatomical structures that work together to perform a certain task. Anatomical systems, such as organ systems, are one organizing principle of anatomy, and can include circulatory, digestive, endocrine, integumentary, immune, lymphatic, muscular, nervous, reproductive, respiratory, skeletal, urinary, vestibular, and other systems.

properties.name: associatedPathophysiology
properties.definition: If applicable, a description of the pathophysiology associated with the anatomical system, including potential abnormal changes in the mechanical, physical, and biochemical functions of the system.

properties.name: comprisedOf
properties.definition: Specifying something physically contained by something else. Typically used here for the underlying anatomical structures, such as organs, that comprise the anatomical system.

properties.name: relatedCondition
properties.definition: A medical condition associated with this anatomy.

properties.name: relatedStructure
properties.definition: Related anatomical structure(s) that are not part of the system but relate or connect to it, such as vascular bundles associated with an organ system.

properties.name: relatedTherapy
properties.definition: A medical therapy related to this anatomy.

classes.name: Article
classes.definition: An article, such as a news article or piece of investigative report. Newspapers and magazines have articles of many different types and this is intended to cover them all.\n\nSee also [blog post](https://blog.schema.org/2014/09/02/schema-org-support-for-bibliographic-relationships-and-periodicals/).

properties.name: articleBody
properties.definition: The actual body of the article.

properties.name: articleSection
properties.definition: Articles may belong to one or more 'sections' in a magazine or newspaper, such as Sports, Lifestyle, etc.

properties.name: backstory
properties.definition: For an [[Article]], typically a [[NewsArticle]], the backstory property provides a textual summary giving a brief explanation of why and how an article was created. In a journalistic setting this could include information about reporting process, methods, interviews, data sources, etc.

properties.name: pageEnd
properties.definition: The page on which the work ends; for example "138" or "xvi".

properties.name: pageStart
properties.definition: The page on which the work starts; for example "135" or "xiii".

properties.name: pagination
properties.definition: Any description of pages that is not separated into pageStart and pageEnd; for example, "1-6, 9, 55" or "10-12, 46-49".

properties.name: speakable
properties.definition:
Indicates sections of a Web page that are particularly 'speakable' in the sense of being highlighted as being especially appropriate for text-to-speech conversion. Other sections of a page may also be usefully spoken in particular circumstances; the 'speakable' property serves to indicate the parts most likely to be generally useful for speech.

The *speakable* property can be repeated an arbitrary number of times, with three kinds of possible 'content-locator' values:

1.) *id-value* URL references - uses *id-value* of an element in the page being annotated. The simplest use of *speakable* has (potentially relative) URL values, referencing identified sections of the document concerned.

2.) CSS Selectors - addresses content in the annotated page, e.g. via class attribute. Use the [[cssSelector]] property.

3.)  XPaths - addresses content via XPaths (assuming an XML view of the content). Use the [[xpath]] property.


For more sophisticated markup of speakable sections beyond simple ID references, either CSS selectors or XPath expressions to pick out document section(s) as speakable. For this
we define a supporting type, [[SpeakableSpecification]]  which is defined to be a possible value of the *speakable* property.

properties.name: wordCount
properties.definition: The number of words in the text of the CreativeWork such as an Article, Book, etc.

classes.name: BioChemEntity
classes.definition: Any biological, chemical, or biochemical thing. For example: a protein; a gene; a chemical; a synthetic chemical.

properties.name: associatedDisease
properties.definition: Disease associated to this BioChemEntity. Such disease can be a MedicalCondition or a URL. If you want to add an evidence supporting the association, please use PropertyValue.

properties.name: bioChemInteraction
properties.definition: A BioChemEntity that is known to interact with this item.

properties.name: bioChemSimilarity
properties.definition: A similar BioChemEntity, e.g., obtained by fingerprint similarity algorithms.

properties.name: biologicalRole
properties.definition: A role played by the BioChemEntity within a biological context.

properties.name: funding
properties.definition: A [[Grant]] that directly or indirectly provide funding or sponsorship for this item. See also [[ownershipFundingInfo]].

properties.name: hasBioChemEntityPart
properties.definition: Indicates a BioChemEntity that (in some sense) has this BioChemEntity as a part.

properties.name: hasMolecularFunction
properties.definition: Molecular function performed by this BioChemEntity; please use PropertyValue if you want to include any evidence.

properties.name: hasRepresentation
properties.definition: A common representation such as a protein sequence or chemical structure for this entity. For images use schema.org/image.

properties.name: isEncodedByBioChemEntity
properties.definition: Another BioChemEntity encoding by this one.

properties.name: isInvolvedInBiologicalProcess
properties.definition: Biological process this BioChemEntity is involved in; please use PropertyValue if you want to include any evidence.

properties.name: isLocatedInSubcellularLocation
properties.definition: Subcellular location where this BioChemEntity is located; please use PropertyValue if you want to include any evidence.

properties.name: isPartOfBioChemEntity
properties.definition: Indicates a BioChemEntity that is (in some sense) a part of this BioChemEntity.

properties.name: taxonomicRange
properties.definition: The taxonomic grouping of the organism that expresses, encodes, or in some way related to the BioChemEntity.

classes.name: BloodTest
classes.definition: A medical test performed on a sample of a patient's blood.

classes.name: BrainStructure
classes.definition: Any anatomical structure which pertains to the soft nervous tissue functioning as the coordinating center of sensation and intellectual and nervous activity.

classes.name: ChemicalSubstance
classes.definition: A chemical substance is 'a portion of matter of constant composition, composed of molecular entities of the same type or of different types' (source: [ChEBI:59999](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/chebi/searchId.do?chebiId=59999)).

properties.name: chemicalComposition
properties.definition: The chemical composition describes the identity and relative ratio of the chemical elements that make up the substance.

properties.name: chemicalRole
properties.definition: A role played by the BioChemEntity within a chemical context.

properties.name: potentialUse
properties.definition: Intended use of the BioChemEntity by humans.

classes.name: ConferenceEvent
classes.definition: Event type: Conference event.

classes.name: CreativeWork
classes.definition: The most generic kind of creative work, including books, movies, photographs, software programs, etc.

properties.name: about
properties.definition: The subject matter of an object.

properties.name: abstract
properties.definition: An abstract is a short description that summarizes a [[CreativeWork]].

properties.name: accessMode
properties.definition: The human sensory perceptual system or cognitive faculty through which a person may process or perceive the intellectual content of a resource, not including any adaptations of the content (e.g., text alternatives for images). Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessMode-vocabulary).

properties.name: accessModeSufficient
properties.definition: A list of single or combined access modes that are sufficient to understand all the intellectual content of a resource, including any adaptations. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessModeSufficient-vocabulary).

properties.name: accessibilityAPI
properties.definition: Indicates that the resource is compatible with the referenced accessibility API. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessibilityAPI-vocabulary).

properties.name: accessibilityControl
properties.definition: Identifies input methods that are sufficient to fully control the described resource. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessibilityControl-vocabulary).

properties.name: accessibilityFeature
properties.definition: Content features of the resource, such as accessible media, alternatives and supported enhancements for accessibility. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessibilityFeature-vocabulary).

properties.name: accessibilityHazard
properties.definition: A characteristic of the described resource that is physiologically dangerous to some users. Related to WCAG 2.0 guideline 2.3. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessibilityHazard-vocabulary).

properties.name: accessibilitySummary
properties.definition: A human-readable summary of specific accessibility features or deficiencies, consistent with the other accessibility metadata but expressing subtleties such as "short descriptions are present but long descriptions will be needed for non-visual users" or "short descriptions are present and no long descriptions are needed".

properties.name: accountablePerson
properties.definition: Specifies the Person that is legally accountable for the CreativeWork.

properties.name: acquireLicensePage
properties.definition: Indicates a page documenting how licenses can be purchased or otherwise acquired, for the current item.

properties.name: aggregateRating
properties.definition: The overall rating, based on a collection of reviews or ratings, of the item.

properties.name: alternativeHeadline
properties.definition: A secondary title of the CreativeWork.

properties.name: archivedAt
properties.definition: Indicates a page or other link involved in archival of a [[CreativeWork]]. In the case of [[MediaReview]], the items in a [[MediaReviewItem]] may often become inaccessible, but be archived by archival, journalistic, activist, or law enforcement organizations. In such cases, the referenced page may not directly publish the content.

properties.name: assesses
properties.definition: The item being described is intended to assess the competency or learning outcome defined by the referenced term.

properties.name: associatedMedia
properties.definition: A media object that encodes this CreativeWork. This property is a synonym for encoding.

properties.name: audience
properties.definition: An intended audience, i.e. a group for whom something was created.

properties.name: audio
properties.definition: An embedded audio object.

properties.name: author
properties.definition: The author of this content or rating. Please note that author is special in that HTML 5 provides a special mechanism for indicating authorship via the rel tag. That is equivalent to this and may be used interchangeably.

properties.name: award
properties.definition: An award won by or for this item.

properties.name: awards
properties.definition: Awards won by or for this item.

properties.name: character
properties.definition: Fictional person connected with a creative work.

properties.name: citation
properties.definition: A citation or reference to another creative work, such as another publication, web page, scholarly article, etc.

properties.name: comment
properties.definition: Comments, typically from users.

properties.name: commentCount
properties.definition: The number of comments this CreativeWork (e.g. Article, Question or Answer) has received. This is most applicable to works published in Web sites with commenting system; additional comments may exist elsewhere.

properties.name: conditionsOfAccess
properties.definition: Conditions that affect the availability of, or method(s) of access to, an item. Typically used for real world items such as an [[ArchiveComponent]] held by an [[ArchiveOrganization]]. This property is not suitable for use as a general Web access control mechanism. It is expressed only in natural language.\n\nFor example "Available by appointment from the Reading Room" or "Accessible only from logged-in accounts ".

properties.name: contentLocation
properties.definition: The location depicted or described in the content. For example, the location in a photograph or painting.

properties.name: contentRating
properties.definition: Official rating of a piece of content—for example, 'MPAA PG-13'.

properties.name: contentReferenceTime
properties.definition: The specific time described by a creative work, for works (e.g. articles, video objects etc.) that emphasise a particular moment within an Event.

properties.name: contributor
properties.definition: A secondary contributor to the CreativeWork or Event.

properties.name: copyrightHolder
properties.definition: The party holding the legal copyright to the CreativeWork.

properties.name: copyrightNotice
properties.definition: Text of a notice appropriate for describing the copyright aspects of this Creative Work, ideally indicating the owner of the copyright for the Work.

properties.name: copyrightYear
properties.definition: The year during which the claimed copyright for the CreativeWork was first asserted.

properties.name: correction
properties.definition: Indicates a correction to a [[CreativeWork]], either via a [[CorrectionComment]], textually or in another document.

properties.name: countryOfOrigin
properties.definition:
The country of origin of something, including products as well as creative  works such as movie and TV content.

In the case of TV and movie, this would be the country of the principle offices of the production company or individual responsible for the movie. For other kinds of [[CreativeWork]] it is difficult to provide fully general guidance, and properties such as [[contentLocation]] and [[locationCreated]] may be more applicable.

In the case of products, the country of origin of the product. The exact interpretation of this may vary by context and product type, and cannot be fully enumerated here.

properties.name: creativeWorkStatus
properties.definition: The status of a creative work in terms of its stage in a lifecycle. Example terms include Incomplete, Draft, Published, Obsolete. Some organizations define a set of terms for the stages of their publication lifecycle.

properties.name: creator
properties.definition: The creator/author of this CreativeWork. This is the same as the Author property for CreativeWork.

properties.name: creditText
properties.definition: Text that can be used to credit person(s) and/or organization(s) associated with a published Creative Work.

properties.name: dateCreated
properties.definition: The date on which the CreativeWork was created or the item was added to a DataFeed.

properties.name: dateModified
properties.definition: The date on which the CreativeWork was most recently modified or when the item's entry was modified within a DataFeed.

properties.name: datePublished
properties.definition: Date of first publication or broadcast. For example the date a [[CreativeWork]] was broadcast or a [[Certification]] was issued.

properties.name: digitalSourceType
properties.definition: Indicates an IPTCDigitalSourceEnumeration code indicating the nature of the digital source(s) for some [[CreativeWork]].

properties.name: discussionUrl
properties.definition: A link to the page containing the comments of the CreativeWork.

properties.name: displayLocation
properties.definition: The location at which an item can be viewed or experienced in-person.

properties.name: editEIDR
properties.definition:
An [EIDR](https://eidr.org/) (Entertainment Identifier Registry) [[identifier]] representing a specific edit / edition for a work of film or television.

For example, the motion picture known as "Ghostbusters" whose [[titleEIDR]] is "10.5240/7EC7-228A-510A-053E-CBB8-J" has several edits, e.g. "10.5240/1F2A-E1C5-680A-14C6-E76B-I" and "10.5240/8A35-3BEE-6497-5D12-9E4F-3".

Since schema.org types like [[Movie]] and [[TVEpisode]] can be used for both works and their multiple expressions, it is possible to use [[titleEIDR]] alone (for a general description), or alongside [[editEIDR]] for a more edit-specific description.

properties.name: editor
properties.definition: Specifies the Person who edited the CreativeWork.

properties.name: educationalAlignment
properties.definition:
An alignment to an established educational framework.

This property should not be used where the nature of the alignment can be described using a simple property, for example to express that a resource [[teaches]] or [[assesses]] a competency.

properties.name: educationalLevel
properties.definition: The level in terms of progression through an educational or training context. Examples of educational levels include 'beginner', 'intermediate' or 'advanced', and formal sets of level indicators.

properties.name: educationalUse
properties.definition: The purpose of a work in the context of education; for example, 'assignment', 'group work'.

properties.name: encoding
properties.definition: A media object that encodes this CreativeWork. This property is a synonym for associatedMedia.

properties.name: encodingFormat
properties.definition:
Media type typically expressed using a MIME format (see [IANA site](http://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml) and [MDN reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Basics_of_HTTP/MIME_types)), e.g. application/zip for a SoftwareApplication binary, audio/mpeg for .mp3 etc.

In cases where a [[CreativeWork]] has several media type representations, [[encoding]] can be used to indicate each [[MediaObject]] alongside particular [[encodingFormat]] information.

Unregistered or niche encoding and file formats can be indicated instead via the most appropriate URL, e.g. defining Web page or a Wikipedia/Wikidata entry.

properties.name: encodings
properties.definition: A media object that encodes this CreativeWork.

properties.name: exampleOfWork
properties.definition: A creative work that this work is an example/instance/realization/derivation of.

properties.name: expires
properties.definition: Date the content expires and is no longer useful or available. For example a [[VideoObject]] or [[NewsArticle]] whose availability or relevance is time-limited, a [[ClaimReview]] fact check whose publisher wants to indicate that it may no longer be relevant (or helpful to highlight) after some date, or a [[Certification]] the validity has expired.

properties.name: fileFormat
properties.definition: Media type, typically MIME format (see [IANA site](http://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml)) of the content, e.g. application/zip of a SoftwareApplication binary. In cases where a CreativeWork has several media type representations, 'encoding' can be used to indicate each MediaObject alongside particular fileFormat information. Unregistered or niche file formats can be indicated instead via the most appropriate URL, e.g. defining Web page or a Wikipedia entry.

properties.name: funder
properties.definition: A person or organization that supports (sponsors) something through some kind of financial contribution.

properties.name: funding
properties.definition: A [[Grant]] that directly or indirectly provide funding or sponsorship for this item. See also [[ownershipFundingInfo]].

properties.name: genre
properties.definition: Genre of the creative work, broadcast channel or group.

properties.name: hasPart
properties.definition: Indicates an item or CreativeWork that is part of this item, or CreativeWork (in some sense).

properties.name: headline
properties.definition: Headline of the article.

properties.name: inLanguage
properties.definition: The language of the content or performance or used in an action. Please use one of the language codes from the [IETF BCP 47 standard](http://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47). See also [[availableLanguage]].

properties.name: interactionStatistic
properties.definition: The number of interactions for the CreativeWork using the WebSite or SoftwareApplication. The most specific child type of InteractionCounter should be used.

properties.name: interactivityType
properties.definition: The predominant mode of learning supported by the learning resource. Acceptable values are 'active', 'expositive', or 'mixed'.

properties.name: interpretedAsClaim
properties.definition: Used to indicate a specific claim contained, implied, translated or refined from the content of a [[MediaObject]] or other [[CreativeWork]]. The interpreting party can be indicated using [[claimInterpreter]].

properties.name: isAccessibleForFree
properties.definition: A flag to signal that the item, event, or place is accessible for free.

properties.name: isBasedOn
properties.definition: A resource from which this work is derived or from which it is a modification or adaptation.

properties.name: isBasedOnUrl
properties.definition: A resource that was used in the creation of this resource. This term can be repeated for multiple sources. For example, http://example.com/great-multiplication-intro.html.

properties.name: isFamilyFriendly
properties.definition: Indicates whether this content is family friendly.

properties.name: isPartOf
properties.definition: Indicates an item or CreativeWork that this item, or CreativeWork (in some sense), is part of.

properties.name: keywords
properties.definition: Keywords or tags used to describe some item. Multiple textual entries in a keywords list are typically delimited by commas, or by repeating the property.

properties.name: learningResourceType
properties.definition: The predominant type or kind characterizing the learning resource. For example, 'presentation', 'handout'.

properties.name: license
properties.definition: A license document that applies to this content, typically indicated by URL.

properties.name: locationCreated
properties.definition: The location where the CreativeWork was created, which may not be the same as the location depicted in the CreativeWork.

properties.name: mainEntity
properties.definition: Indicates the primary entity described in some page or other CreativeWork.

properties.name: maintainer
properties.definition: A maintainer of a [[Dataset]], software package ([[SoftwareApplication]]), or other [[Project]]. A maintainer is a [[Person]] or [[Organization]] that manages contributions to, and/or publication of, some (typically complex) artifact. It is common for distributions of software and data to be based on "upstream" sources. When [[maintainer]] is applied to a specific version of something e.g. a particular version or packaging of a [[Dataset]], it is always  possible that the upstream source has a different maintainer. The [[isBasedOn]] property can be used to indicate such relationships between datasets to make the different maintenance roles clear. Similarly in the case of software, a package may have dedicated maintainers working on integration into software distributions such as Ubuntu, as well as upstream maintainers of the underlying work.

properties.name: material
properties.definition: A material that something is made from, e.g. leather, wool, cotton, paper.

properties.name: materialExtent
properties.definition: The quantity of the materials being described or an expression of the physical space they occupy.

properties.name: mentions
properties.definition: Indicates that the CreativeWork contains a reference to, but is not necessarily about a concept.

properties.name: offers
properties.definition: An offer to provide this item—for example, an offer to sell a product, rent the DVD of a movie, perform a service, or give away tickets to an event. Use [[businessFunction]] to indicate the kind of transaction offered, i.e. sell, lease, etc. This property can also be used to describe a [[Demand]]. While this property is listed as expected on a number of common types, it can be used in others. In that case, using a second type, such as Product or a subtype of Product, can clarify the nature of the offer.

properties.name: pattern
properties.definition: A pattern that something has, for example 'polka dot', 'striped', 'Canadian flag'. Values are typically expressed as text, although links to controlled value schemes are also supported.

properties.name: position
properties.definition: The position of an item in a series or sequence of items.

properties.name: producer
properties.definition: The person or organization who produced the work (e.g. music album, movie, TV/radio series etc.).

properties.name: provider
properties.definition: The service provider, service operator, or service performer; the goods producer. Another party (a seller) may offer those services or goods on behalf of the provider. A provider may also serve as the seller.

properties.name: publication
properties.definition: A publication event associated with the item.

properties.name: publisher
properties.definition: The publisher of the article in question.

properties.name: publisherImprint
properties.definition: The publishing division which published the comic.

properties.name: publishingPrinciples
properties.definition:
The publishingPrinciples property indicates (typically via [[URL]]) a document describing the editorial principles of an [[Organization]] (or individual, e.g. a [[Person]] writing a blog) that relate to their activities as a publisher, e.g. ethics or diversity policies. When applied to a [[CreativeWork]] (e.g. [[NewsArticle]]) the principles are those of the party primarily responsible for the creation of the [[CreativeWork]].

While such policies are most typically expressed in natural language, sometimes related information (e.g. indicating a [[funder]]) can be expressed using schema.org terminology.

properties.name: recordedAt
properties.definition: The Event where the CreativeWork was recorded. The CreativeWork may capture all or part of the event.

properties.name: releasedEvent
properties.definition: The place and time the release was issued, expressed as a PublicationEvent.

properties.name: review
properties.definition: A review of the item.

properties.name: reviews
properties.definition: Review of the item.

properties.name: schemaVersion
properties.definition:
Indicates (by URL or string) a particular version of a schema used in some CreativeWork. This property was created primarily to
    indicate the use of a specific schema.org release, e.g. ```10.0``` as a simple string, or more explicitly via URL, ```https://schema.org/docs/releases.html#v10.0```. There may be situations in which other schemas might usefully be referenced this way, e.g. ```http://dublincore.org/specifications/dublin-core/dces/1999-07-02/``` but this has not been carefully explored in the community.

properties.name: sdDatePublished
properties.definition: Indicates the date on which the current structured data was generated / published. Typically used alongside [[sdPublisher]].

properties.name: sdLicense
properties.definition: A license document that applies to this structured data, typically indicated by URL.

properties.name: sdPublisher
properties.definition:
Indicates the party responsible for generating and publishing the current structured data markup, typically in cases where the structured data is derived automatically from existing published content but published on a different site. For example, student projects and open data initiatives often re-publish existing content with more explicitly structured metadata. The
[[sdPublisher]] property helps make such practices more explicit.

properties.name: size
properties.definition: A standardized size of a product or creative work, specified either through a simple textual string (for example 'XL', '32Wx34L'), a  QuantitativeValue with a unitCode, or a comprehensive and structured [[SizeSpecification]]; in other cases, the [[width]], [[height]], [[depth]] and [[weight]] properties may be more applicable.

properties.name: sourceOrganization
properties.definition: The Organization on whose behalf the creator was working.

properties.name: spatial
properties.definition:
The "spatial" property can be used in cases when more specific properties
(e.g. [[locationCreated]], [[spatialCoverage]], [[contentLocation]]) are not known to be appropriate.

properties.name: spatialCoverage
properties.definition:
The spatialCoverage of a CreativeWork indicates the place(s) which are the focus of the content. It is a subproperty of
      contentLocation intended primarily for more technical and detailed materials. For example with a Dataset, it indicates
      areas that the dataset describes: a dataset of New York weather would have spatialCoverage which was the place: the state of New York.

properties.name: sponsor
properties.definition: A person or organization that supports a thing through a pledge, promise, or financial contribution. E.g. a sponsor of a Medical Study or a corporate sponsor of an event.

properties.name: teaches
properties.definition: The item being described is intended to help a person learn the competency or learning outcome defined by the referenced term.

properties.name: temporal
properties.definition:
The "temporal" property can be used in cases where more specific properties
(e.g. [[temporalCoverage]], [[dateCreated]], [[dateModified]], [[datePublished]]) are not known to be appropriate.

properties.name: temporalCoverage
properties.definition:
The temporalCoverage of a CreativeWork indicates the period that the content applies to, i.e. that it describes, either as a DateTime or as a textual string indicating a time period in [ISO 8601 time interval format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Time_intervals). In
      the case of a Dataset it will typically indicate the relevant time period in a precise notation (e.g. for a 2011 census dataset, the year 2011 would be written "2011/2012"). Other forms of content, e.g. ScholarlyArticle, Book, TVSeries or TVEpisode, may indicate their temporalCoverage in broader terms - textually or via well-known URL.
      Written works such as books may sometimes have precise temporal coverage too, e.g. a work set in 1939 - 1945 can be indicated in ISO 8601 interval format format via "1939/1945".

Open-ended date ranges can be written with ".." in place of the end date. For example, "2015-11/.." indicates a range beginning in November 2015 and with no specified final date. This is tentative and might be updated in future when ISO 8601 is officially updated.

properties.name: text
properties.definition: The textual content of this CreativeWork.

properties.name: thumbnail
properties.definition: Thumbnail image for an image or video.

properties.name: thumbnailUrl
properties.definition: A thumbnail image relevant to the Thing.

properties.name: timeRequired
properties.definition: Approximate or typical time it usually takes to work with or through the content of this work for the typical or target audience.

properties.name: translationOfWork
properties.definition: The work that this work has been translated from. E.g. 物种起源 is a translationOf “On the Origin of Species”.

properties.name: translator
properties.definition: Organization or person who adapts a creative work to different languages, regional differences and technical requirements of a target market, or that translates during some event.

properties.name: typicalAgeRange
properties.definition: The typical expected age range, e.g. '7-9', '11-'.

properties.name: usageInfo
properties.definition:
The schema.org [[usageInfo]] property indicates further information about a [[CreativeWork]]. This property is applicable both to works that are freely available and to those that require payment or other transactions. It can reference additional information, e.g. community expectations on preferred linking and citation conventions, as well as purchasing details. For something that can be commercially licensed, usageInfo can provide detailed, resource-specific information about licensing options.

This property can be used alongside the license property which indicates license(s) applicable to some piece of content. The usageInfo property can provide information about other licensing options, e.g. acquiring commercial usage rights for an image that is also available under non-commercial creative commons licenses.

properties.name: version
properties.definition: The version of the CreativeWork embodied by a specified resource.

properties.name: video
properties.definition: An embedded video object.

properties.name: wordCount
properties.definition: The number of words in the text of the CreativeWork such as an Article, Book, etc.

properties.name: workExample
properties.definition: Example/instance/realization/derivation of the concept of this creative work. E.g. the paperback edition, first edition, or e-book.

properties.name: workTranslation
properties.definition: A work that is a translation of the content of this work. E.g. 西遊記 has an English workTranslation “Journey to the West”, a German workTranslation “Monkeys Pilgerfahrt” and a Vietnamese  translation Tây du ký bình khảo.

classes.name: Dataset
classes.definition: A body of structured information describing some topic(s) of interest.

properties.name: catalog
properties.definition: A data catalog which contains this dataset.

properties.name: datasetTimeInterval
properties.definition: The range of temporal applicability of a dataset, e.g. for a 2011 census dataset, the year 2011 (in ISO 8601 time interval format).

properties.name: distribution
properties.definition: A downloadable form of this dataset, at a specific location, in a specific format. This property can be repeated if different variations are available. There is no expectation that different downloadable distributions must contain exactly equivalent information (see also [DCAT](https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dcat-3/#Class:Distribution) on this point). Different distributions might include or exclude different subsets of the entire dataset, for example.

properties.name: includedDataCatalog
properties.definition: A data catalog which contains this dataset (this property was previously 'catalog', preferred name is now 'includedInDataCatalog').

properties.name: includedInDataCatalog
properties.definition: A data catalog which contains this dataset.

properties.name: issn
properties.definition: The International Standard Serial Number (ISSN) that identifies this serial publication. You can repeat this property to identify different formats of, or the linking ISSN (ISSN-L) for, this serial publication.

properties.name: measurementMethod
properties.definition: A subproperty of [[measurementTechnique]] that can be used for specifying specific methods, in particular via [[MeasurementMethodEnum]].

properties.name: measurementTechnique
properties.definition:
A technique, method or technology used in an [[Observation]], [[StatisticalVariable]] or [[Dataset]] (or [[DataDownload]], [[DataCatalog]]), corresponding to the method used for measuring the corresponding variable(s) (for datasets, described using [[variableMeasured]]; for [[Observation]], a [[StatisticalVariable]]). Often but not necessarily each [[variableMeasured]] will have an explicit representation as (or mapping to) an property such as those defined in Schema.org, or other RDF vocabularies and "knowledge graphs". In that case the subproperty of [[variableMeasured]] called [[measuredProperty]] is applicable.
    
The [[measurementTechnique]] property helps when extra clarification is needed about how a [[measuredProperty]] was measured. This is oriented towards scientific and scholarly dataset publication but may have broader applicability; it is not intended as a full representation of measurement, but can often serve as a high level summary for dataset discovery. 

For example, if [[variableMeasured]] is: molecule concentration, [[measurementTechnique]] could be: "mass spectrometry" or "nmr spectroscopy" or "colorimetry" or "immunofluorescence". If the [[variableMeasured]] is "depression rating", the [[measurementTechnique]] could be "Zung Scale" or "HAM-D" or "Beck Depression Inventory". 

If there are several [[variableMeasured]] properties recorded for some given data object, use a [[PropertyValue]] for each [[variableMeasured]] and attach the corresponding [[measurementTechnique]]. The value can also be from an enumeration, organized as a [[MeasurementMethodEnum]].

properties.name: variableMeasured
properties.definition: The variableMeasured property can indicate (repeated as necessary) the  variables that are measured in some dataset, either described as text or as pairs of identifier and description using PropertyValue, or more explicitly as a [[StatisticalVariable]].

classes.name: DiagnosticProcedure
classes.definition: A medical procedure intended primarily for diagnostic, as opposed to therapeutic, purposes.

classes.name: Drug
classes.definition: A chemical or biologic substance, used as a medical therapy, that has a physiological effect on an organism. Here the term drug is used interchangeably with the term medicine although clinical knowledge makes a clear difference between them.

properties.name: activeIngredient
properties.definition: An active ingredient, typically chemical compounds and/or biologic substances.

properties.name: administrationRoute
properties.definition: A route by which this drug may be administered, e.g. 'oral'.

properties.name: alcoholWarning
properties.definition: Any precaution, guidance, contraindication, etc. related to consumption of alcohol while taking this drug.

properties.name: availableStrength
properties.definition: An available dosage strength for the drug.

properties.name: breastfeedingWarning
properties.definition: Any precaution, guidance, contraindication, etc. related to this drug's use by breastfeeding mothers.

properties.name: clincalPharmacology
properties.definition: Description of the absorption and elimination of drugs, including their concentration (pharmacokinetics, pK) and biological effects (pharmacodynamics, pD).

properties.name: clinicalPharmacology
properties.definition: Description of the absorption and elimination of drugs, including their concentration (pharmacokinetics, pK) and biological effects (pharmacodynamics, pD).

properties.name: dosageForm
properties.definition: A dosage form in which this drug/supplement is available, e.g. 'tablet', 'suspension', 'injection'.

properties.name: doseSchedule
properties.definition: A dosing schedule for the drug for a given population, either observed, recommended, or maximum dose based on the type used.

properties.name: drugClass
properties.definition: The class of drug this belongs to (e.g., statins).

properties.name: drugUnit
properties.definition: The unit in which the drug is measured, e.g. '5 mg tablet'.

properties.name: foodWarning
properties.definition: Any precaution, guidance, contraindication, etc. related to consumption of specific foods while taking this drug.

properties.name: includedInHealthInsurancePlan
properties.definition: The insurance plans that cover this drug.

properties.name: interactingDrug
properties.definition: Another drug that is known to interact with this drug in a way that impacts the effect of this drug or causes a risk to the patient. Note: disease interactions are typically captured as contraindications.

properties.name: isAvailableGenerically
properties.definition: True if the drug is available in a generic form (regardless of name).

properties.name: isProprietary
properties.definition: True if this item's name is a proprietary/brand name (vs. generic name).

properties.name: labelDetails
properties.definition: Link to the drug's label details.

properties.name: legalStatus
properties.definition: The drug or supplement's legal status, including any controlled substance schedules that apply.

properties.name: maximumIntake
properties.definition: Recommended intake of this supplement for a given population as defined by a specific recommending authority.

properties.name: mechanismOfAction
properties.definition: The specific biochemical interaction through which this drug or supplement produces its pharmacological effect.

properties.name: nonProprietaryName
properties.definition: The generic name of this drug or supplement.

properties.name: overdosage
properties.definition: Any information related to overdose on a drug, including signs or symptoms, treatments, contact information for emergency response.

properties.name: pregnancyCategory
properties.definition: Pregnancy category of this drug.

properties.name: pregnancyWarning
properties.definition: Any precaution, guidance, contraindication, etc. related to this drug's use during pregnancy.

properties.name: prescribingInfo
properties.definition: Link to prescribing information for the drug.

properties.name: prescriptionStatus
properties.definition: Indicates the status of drug prescription, e.g. local catalogs classifications or whether the drug is available by prescription or over-the-counter, etc.

properties.name: proprietaryName
properties.definition: Proprietary name given to the diet plan, typically by its originator or creator.

properties.name: relatedDrug
properties.definition: Any other drug related to this one, for example commonly-prescribed alternatives.

properties.name: rxcui
properties.definition: The RxCUI drug identifier from RXNORM.

properties.name: warning
properties.definition: Any FDA or other warnings about the drug (text or URL).

classes.name: EducationEvent
classes.definition: Event type: Education event.

properties.name: assesses
properties.definition: The item being described is intended to assess the competency or learning outcome defined by the referenced term.

properties.name: educationalLevel
properties.definition: The level in terms of progression through an educational or training context. Examples of educational levels include 'beginner', 'intermediate' or 'advanced', and formal sets of level indicators.

properties.name: teaches
properties.definition: The item being described is intended to help a person learn the competency or learning outcome defined by the referenced term.

classes.name: Event
classes.definition: An event happening at a certain time and location, such as a concert, lecture, or festival. Ticketing information may be added via the [[offers]] property. Repeated events may be structured as separate Event objects.

properties.name: about
properties.definition: The subject matter of an object.

properties.name: actor
properties.definition: An actor (individual or a group), e.g. in TV, radio, movie, video games etc., or in an event. Actors can be associated with individual items or with a series, episode, clip.

properties.name: aggregateRating
properties.definition: The overall rating, based on a collection of reviews or ratings, of the item.

properties.name: attendee
properties.definition: A person or organization attending the event.

properties.name: attendees
properties.definition: A person attending the event.

properties.name: audience
properties.definition: An intended audience, i.e. a group for whom something was created.

properties.name: composer
properties.definition: The person or organization who wrote a composition, or who is the composer of a work performed at some event.

properties.name: contributor
properties.definition: A secondary contributor to the CreativeWork or Event.

properties.name: director
properties.definition: A director of e.g. TV, radio, movie, video gaming etc. content, or of an event. Directors can be associated with individual items or with a series, episode, clip.

properties.name: doorTime
properties.definition: The time admission will commence.

properties.name: duration
properties.definition: The duration of the item (movie, audio recording, event, etc.) in [ISO 8601 duration format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

properties.name: endDate
properties.definition: The end date and time of the item (in [ISO 8601 date format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)).

properties.name: eventAttendanceMode
properties.definition: The eventAttendanceMode of an event indicates whether it occurs online, offline, or a mix.

properties.name: eventSchedule
properties.definition:
Associates an [[Event]] with a [[Schedule]]. There are circumstances where it is preferable to share a schedule for a series of
      repeating events rather than data on the individual events themselves. For example, a website or application might prefer to publish a schedule for a weekly
      gym class rather than provide data on every event. A schedule could be processed by applications to add forthcoming events to a calendar. An [[Event]] that
      is associated with a [[Schedule]] using this property should not have [[startDate]] or [[endDate]] properties. These are instead defined within the associated
      [[Schedule]], this avoids any ambiguity for clients using the data. The property might have repeated values to specify different schedules, e.g. for different months
      or seasons.

properties.name: eventStatus
properties.definition: An eventStatus of an event represents its status; particularly useful when an event is cancelled or rescheduled.

properties.name: funder
properties.definition: A person or organization that supports (sponsors) something through some kind of financial contribution.

properties.name: funding
properties.definition: A [[Grant]] that directly or indirectly provide funding or sponsorship for this item. See also [[ownershipFundingInfo]].

properties.name: hasParticipationOffer
properties.definition: An offer to participate in the event, for example, Call for Proposals, Call for Speakers, or Call for Performers.

properties.name: hasSponsorshipOffer
properties.definition: An offer to sponsor the event, for example, Sponsorship Prospectus, Sponsorship Opportunities, or Sponsor Packages.

properties.name: inLanguage
properties.definition: The language of the content or performance or used in an action. Please use one of the language codes from the [IETF BCP 47 standard](http://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47). See also [[availableLanguage]].

properties.name: isAccessibleForFree
properties.definition: A flag to signal that the item, event, or place is accessible for free.

properties.name: keywords
properties.definition: Keywords or tags used to describe some item. Multiple textual entries in a keywords list are typically delimited by commas, or by repeating the property.

properties.name: location
properties.definition: The location of, for example, where an event is happening, where an organization is located, or where an action takes place.

properties.name: maximumAttendeeCapacity
properties.definition: The total number of individuals that may attend an event or venue.

properties.name: maximumPhysicalAttendeeCapacity
properties.definition: The maximum physical attendee capacity of an [[Event]] whose [[eventAttendanceMode]] is [[OfflineEventAttendanceMode]] (or the offline aspects, in the case of a [[MixedEventAttendanceMode]]).

properties.name: maximumVirtualAttendeeCapacity
properties.definition: The maximum virtual attendee capacity of an [[Event]] whose [[eventAttendanceMode]] is [[OnlineEventAttendanceMode]] (or the online aspects, in the case of a [[MixedEventAttendanceMode]]).

properties.name: offers
properties.definition: An offer to provide this item—for example, an offer to sell a product, rent the DVD of a movie, perform a service, or give away tickets to an event. Use [[businessFunction]] to indicate the kind of transaction offered, i.e. sell, lease, etc. This property can also be used to describe a [[Demand]]. While this property is listed as expected on a number of common types, it can be used in others. In that case, using a second type, such as Product or a subtype of Product, can clarify the nature of the offer.

properties.name: organizer
properties.definition: An organizer of an Event.

properties.name: performer
properties.definition: A performer at the event—for example, a presenter, musician, musical group or actor.

properties.name: performers
properties.definition: The main performer or performers of the event—for example, a presenter, musician, or actor.

properties.name: previousStartDate
properties.definition: Used in conjunction with eventStatus for rescheduled or cancelled events. This property contains the previously scheduled start date. For rescheduled events, the startDate property should be used for the newly scheduled start date. In the (rare) case of an event that has been postponed and rescheduled multiple times, this field may be repeated.

properties.name: recordedIn
properties.definition: The CreativeWork that captured all or part of this Event.

properties.name: remainingAttendeeCapacity
properties.definition: The number of attendee places for an event that remain unallocated.

properties.name: review
properties.definition: A review of the item.

properties.name: sponsor
properties.definition: A person or organization that supports a thing through a pledge, promise, or financial contribution. E.g. a sponsor of a Medical Study or a corporate sponsor of an event.

properties.name: startDate
properties.definition: The start date and time of the item (in [ISO 8601 date format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)).

properties.name: subEvent
properties.definition: An Event that is part of this event. For example, a conference event includes many presentations, each of which is a subEvent of the conference.

properties.name: subEvents
properties.definition: Events that are a part of this event. For example, a conference event includes many presentations, each subEvents of the conference.

properties.name: superEvent
properties.definition: An event that this event is a part of. For example, a collection of individual music performances might each have a music festival as their superEvent.

properties.name: translator
properties.definition: Organization or person who adapts a creative work to different languages, regional differences and technical requirements of a target market, or that translates during some event.

properties.name: typicalAgeRange
properties.definition: The typical expected age range, e.g. '7-9', '11-'.

properties.name: workFeatured
properties.definition:
A work featured in some event, e.g. exhibited in an ExhibitionEvent.
       Specific subproperties are available for workPerformed (e.g. a play), or a workPresented (a Movie at a ScreeningEvent).

properties.name: workPerformed
properties.definition: A work performed in some event, for example a play performed in a TheaterEvent.

classes.name: Gene
classes.definition: A discrete unit of inheritance which affects one or more biological traits (Source: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gene](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gene)). Examples include FOXP2 (Forkhead box protein P2), SCARNA21 (small Cajal body-specific RNA 21), A- (agouti genotype).

properties.name: alternativeOf
properties.definition: Another gene which is a variation of this one.

properties.name: encodesBioChemEntity
properties.definition: Another BioChemEntity encoded by this one.

properties.name: expressedIn
properties.definition: Tissue, organ, biological sample, etc in which activity of this gene has been observed experimentally. For example brain, digestive system.

properties.name: hasBioPolymerSequence
properties.definition: A symbolic representation of a BioChemEntity. For example, a nucleotide sequence of a Gene or an amino acid sequence of a Protein.

classes.name: ImagingTest
classes.definition: Any medical imaging modality typically used for diagnostic purposes.

properties.name: imagingTechnique
properties.definition: Imaging technique used.

classes.name: InfectiousDisease
classes.definition: An infectious disease is a clinically evident human disease resulting from the presence of pathogenic microbial agents, like pathogenic viruses, pathogenic bacteria, fungi, protozoa, multicellular parasites, and prions. To be considered an infectious disease, such pathogens are known to be able to cause this disease.

properties.name: infectiousAgent
properties.definition: The actual infectious agent, such as a specific bacterium.

properties.name: infectiousAgentClass
properties.definition: The class of infectious agent (bacteria, prion, etc.) that causes the disease.

properties.name: transmissionMethod
properties.definition: How the disease spreads, either as a route or vector, for example 'direct contact', 'Aedes aegypti', etc.

classes.name: MedicalCondition
classes.definition: Any condition of the human body that affects the normal functioning of a person, whether physically or mentally. Includes diseases, injuries, disabilities, disorders, syndromes, etc.

properties.name: associatedAnatomy
properties.definition: The anatomy of the underlying organ system or structures associated with this entity.

properties.name: cause
properties.definition: The cause of a medical condition.

properties.name: differentialDiagnosis
properties.definition: One of a set of differential diagnoses for the condition. Specifically, a closely-related or competing diagnosis typically considered later in the cognitive process whereby this medical condition is distinguished from others most likely responsible for a similar collection of signs and symptoms to reach the most parsimonious diagnosis or diagnoses in a patient.

properties.name: drug
properties.definition: Specifying a drug or medicine used in a medication procedure.

properties.name: epidemiology
properties.definition: The characteristics of associated patients, such as age, gender, race etc.

properties.name: expectedPrognosis
properties.definition: The likely outcome in either the short term or long term of the medical condition.

properties.name: naturalProgression
properties.definition: The expected progression of the condition if it is not treated and allowed to progress naturally.

properties.name: pathophysiology
properties.definition: Changes in the normal mechanical, physical, and biochemical functions that are associated with this activity or condition.

properties.name: possibleComplication
properties.definition: A possible unexpected and unfavorable evolution of a medical condition. Complications may include worsening of the signs or symptoms of the disease, extension of the condition to other organ systems, etc.

properties.name: possibleTreatment
properties.definition: A possible treatment to address this condition, sign or symptom.

properties.name: primaryPrevention
properties.definition: A preventative therapy used to prevent an initial occurrence of the medical condition, such as vaccination.

properties.name: riskFactor
properties.definition: A modifiable or non-modifiable factor that increases the risk of a patient contracting this condition, e.g. age,  coexisting condition.

properties.name: secondaryPrevention
properties.definition: A preventative therapy used to prevent reoccurrence of the medical condition after an initial episode of the condition.

properties.name: signOrSymptom
properties.definition: A sign or symptom of this condition. Signs are objective or physically observable manifestations of the medical condition while symptoms are the subjective experience of the medical condition.

properties.name: stage
properties.definition: The stage of the condition, if applicable.

properties.name: status
properties.definition: The status of the study (enumerated).

properties.name: typicalTest
properties.definition: A medical test typically performed given this condition.

classes.name: MedicalDevice
classes.definition: Any object used in a medical capacity, such as to diagnose or treat a patient.

properties.name: adverseOutcome
properties.definition: A possible complication and/or side effect of this therapy. If it is known that an adverse outcome is serious (resulting in death, disability, or permanent damage; requiring hospitalization; or otherwise life-threatening or requiring immediate medical attention), tag it as a seriousAdverseOutcome instead.

properties.name: contraindication
properties.definition: A contraindication for this therapy.

properties.name: postOp
properties.definition: A description of the postoperative procedures, care, and/or followups for this device.

properties.name: preOp
properties.definition: A description of the workup, testing, and other preparations required before implanting this device.

properties.name: procedure
properties.definition: A description of the procedure involved in setting up, using, and/or installing the device.

properties.name: seriousAdverseOutcome
properties.definition: A possible serious complication and/or serious side effect of this therapy. Serious adverse outcomes include those that are life-threatening; result in death, disability, or permanent damage; require hospitalization or prolong existing hospitalization; cause congenital anomalies or birth defects; or jeopardize the patient and may require medical or surgical intervention to prevent one of the outcomes in this definition.

classes.name: MedicalEntity
classes.definition: The most generic type of entity related to health and the practice of medicine.

properties.name: code
properties.definition: A medical code for the entity, taken from a controlled vocabulary or ontology such as ICD-9, DiseasesDB, MeSH, SNOMED-CT, RxNorm, etc.

properties.name: funding
properties.definition: A [[Grant]] that directly or indirectly provide funding or sponsorship for this item. See also [[ownershipFundingInfo]].

properties.name: guideline
properties.definition: A medical guideline related to this entity.

properties.name: legalStatus
properties.definition: The drug or supplement's legal status, including any controlled substance schedules that apply.

properties.name: medicineSystem
properties.definition: The system of medicine that includes this MedicalEntity, for example 'evidence-based', 'homeopathic', 'chiropractic', etc.

properties.name: recognizingAuthority
properties.definition: If applicable, the organization that officially recognizes this entity as part of its endorsed system of medicine.

properties.name: relevantSpecialty
properties.definition: If applicable, a medical specialty in which this entity is relevant.

properties.name: study
properties.definition: A medical study or trial related to this entity.

classes.name: MedicalObservationalStudy
classes.definition: An observational study is a type of medical study that attempts to infer the possible effect of a treatment through observation of a cohort of subjects over a period of time. In an observational study, the assignment of subjects into treatment groups versus control groups is outside the control of the investigator. This is in contrast with controlled studies, such as the randomized controlled trials represented by MedicalTrial, where each subject is randomly assigned to a treatment group or a control group before the start of the treatment.

properties.name: studyDesign
properties.definition: Specifics about the observational study design (enumerated).

classes.name: MedicalProcedure
classes.definition: A process of care used in either a diagnostic, therapeutic, preventive or palliative capacity that relies on invasive (surgical), non-invasive, or other techniques.

properties.name: bodyLocation
properties.definition: Location in the body of the anatomical structure.

properties.name: followup
properties.definition: Typical or recommended followup care after the procedure is performed.

properties.name: howPerformed
properties.definition: How the procedure is performed.

properties.name: preparation
properties.definition: Typical preparation that a patient must undergo before having the procedure performed.

properties.name: procedureType
properties.definition: The type of procedure, for example Surgical, Noninvasive, or Percutaneous.

properties.name: status
properties.definition: The status of the study (enumerated).

classes.name: MedicalSignOrSymptom
classes.definition: Any feature associated or not with a medical condition. In medicine a symptom is generally subjective while a sign is objective.

properties.name: possibleTreatment
properties.definition: A possible treatment to address this condition, sign or symptom.

classes.name: MedicalStudy
classes.definition: A medical study is an umbrella type covering all kinds of research studies relating to human medicine or health, including observational studies and interventional trials and registries, randomized, controlled or not. When the specific type of study is known, use one of the extensions of this type, such as MedicalTrial or MedicalObservationalStudy. Also, note that this type should be used to mark up data that describes the study itself; to tag an article that publishes the results of a study, use MedicalScholarlyArticle. Note: use the code property of MedicalEntity to store study IDs, e.g. clinicaltrials.gov ID.

properties.name: healthCondition
properties.definition: Specifying the health condition(s) of a patient, medical study, or other target audience.

properties.name: sponsor
properties.definition: A person or organization that supports a thing through a pledge, promise, or financial contribution. E.g. a sponsor of a Medical Study or a corporate sponsor of an event.

properties.name: status
properties.definition: The status of the study (enumerated).

properties.name: studyLocation
properties.definition: The location in which the study is taking/took place.

properties.name: studySubject
properties.definition: A subject of the study, i.e. one of the medical conditions, therapies, devices, drugs, etc. investigated by the study.

classes.name: MedicalTest
classes.definition: Any medical test, typically performed for diagnostic purposes.

properties.name: affectedBy
properties.definition: Drugs that affect the test's results.

properties.name: normalRange
properties.definition: Range of acceptable values for a typical patient, when applicable.

properties.name: signDetected
properties.definition: A sign detected by the test.

properties.name: usedToDiagnose
properties.definition: A condition the test is used to diagnose.

properties.name: usesDevice
properties.definition: Device used to perform the test.

classes.name: MedicalTrial
classes.definition: A medical trial is a type of medical study that uses a scientific process to compare the safety and efficacy of medical therapies or medical procedures. In general, medical trials are controlled and subjects are allocated at random to the different treatment and/or control groups.

properties.name: trialDesign
properties.definition: Specifics about the trial design (enumerated).

classes.name: MolecularEntity
classes.definition: Any constitutionally or isotopically distinct atom, molecule, ion, ion pair, radical, radical ion, complex, conformer etc., identifiable as a separately distinguishable entity.

properties.name: chemicalRole
properties.definition: A role played by the BioChemEntity within a chemical context.

properties.name: inChI
properties.definition: Non-proprietary identifier for molecular entity that can be used in printed and electronic data sources thus enabling easier linking of diverse data compilations.

properties.name: inChIKey
properties.definition: InChIKey is a hashed version of the full InChI (using the SHA-256 algorithm).

properties.name: iupacName
properties.definition: Systematic method of naming chemical compounds as recommended by the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC).

properties.name: molecularFormula
properties.definition: The empirical formula is the simplest whole number ratio of all the atoms in a molecule.

properties.name: molecularWeight
properties.definition: This is the molecular weight of the entity being described, not of the parent. Units should be included in the form '<Number> <unit>', for example '12 amu' or as '<QuantitativeValue>.

properties.name: monoisotopicMolecularWeight
properties.definition: The monoisotopic mass is the sum of the masses of the atoms in a molecule using the unbound, ground-state, rest mass of the principal (most abundant) isotope for each element instead of the isotopic average mass. Please include the units in the form '<Number> <unit>', for example '770.230488 g/mol' or as '<QuantitativeValue>.

properties.name: potentialUse
properties.definition: Intended use of the BioChemEntity by humans.

properties.name: smiles
properties.definition: A specification in form of a line notation for describing the structure of chemical species using short ASCII strings.  Double bond stereochemistry \ indicators may need to be escaped in the string in formats where the backslash is an escape character.

classes.name: Nerve
classes.definition: A common pathway for the electrochemical nerve impulses that are transmitted along each of the axons.

properties.name: branch
properties.definition: The branches that delineate from the nerve bundle. Not to be confused with [[branchOf]].

properties.name: hasGS1DigitalLink
properties.definition:
The GS1 digital link associated with the object. This URL should conform to the particular requirements of digital links. The link should only contain the Application Identifiers (AIs) that are relevant for the entity being annotated, for instance a [[Product]] or an [[Organization]], and for the correct granularity. In particular, for products:
  • A Digital Link that contains a serial number (AI 21) should only be present on instances of [[IndividualProduct]]
  • A Digital Link that contains a lot number (AI 10) should be annotated as [[SomeProducts]] if only products from that lot are sold, or [[IndividualProduct]] if there is only a specific product.
  • A Digital Link that contains a global model number (AI 8013) should be attached to a [[Product]] or a [[ProductModel]].
Other item types should be adapted similarly.

properties.name: iso6523Code
properties.definition: An organization identifier as defined in [ISO 6523(-1)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO/IEC_6523). The identifier should be in the `XXXX:YYYYYY:ZZZ` or `XXXX:YYYYYY`format. Where `XXXX` is a 4 digit _ICD_ (International Code Designator), `YYYYYY` is an _OID_ (Organization Identifier) with all formatting characters (dots, dashes, spaces) removed with a maximal length of 35 characters, and `ZZZ` is an optional OPI (Organization Part Identifier) with a maximum length of 35 characters. The various components (ICD, OID, OPI) are joined with a colon character (ASCII `0x3a`). Note that many existing organization identifiers defined as attributes like [leiCode](https://schema.org/leiCode) (`0199`), [duns](https://schema.org/duns) (`0060`) or [GLN](https://schema.org/globalLocationNumber) (`0088`) can be expressed using ISO-6523. If possible, ISO-6523 codes should be preferred to populating [vatID](https://schema.org/vatID) or [taxID](https://schema.org/taxID), as ISO identifiers are less ambiguous.

When applied to a [[CreativeWork]] (e.g. [[NewsArticle]]) the principles are those of the party primarily responsible for the creation of the [[CreativeWork]].

properties.name: pronouns
properties.definition:
A short string listing or describing pronouns for a person. Typically the person concerned is the best authority as pronouns are a critical part of personal identity and expression. Publishers and consumers of this information are reminded to treat this data responsibly, take country-specific laws related to gender expression into account, and be wary of out-of-date data and drawing unwarranted inferences about the person being described.

In English, formulations such as "they/them", "she/her", and "he/him" are commonly used online and can also be used here. We do not intend to enumerate all possible micro-syntaxes in all languages. More structured and well-defined external values for pronouns can be referenced using the [[StructuredValue]] or [[DefinedTerm]] values.

The code is typically assigned by the parentOrganization and used in structured URLs.\n\nFor example, in the URL http://www.starbucks.co.uk/store-locator/etc/detail/3047 the code "3047" is a branchCode for a particular branch.

properties.name: geoContains
properties.definition: Represents a relationship between two geometries (or the places they represent), relating a containing geometry to a contained geometry. "a contains b iff no points of b lie in the exterior of a, and at least one point of the interior of b lies in the interior of a". As defined in [DE-9IM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DE-9IM).

properties.name: geoCoveredBy
properties.definition: Represents a relationship between two geometries (or the places they represent), relating a geometry to another that covers it. As defined in [DE-9IM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DE-9IM).

properties.name: geoCovers
properties.definition: Represents a relationship between two geometries (or the places they represent), relating a covering geometry to a covered geometry. "Every point of b is a point of (the interior or boundary of) a". As defined in [DE-9IM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DE-9IM).

properties.name: geoCrosses
properties.definition: Represents a relationship between two geometries (or the places they represent), relating a geometry to another that crosses it: "a crosses b: they have some but not all interior points in common, and the dimension of the intersection is less than that of at least one of them". As defined in [DE-9IM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DE-9IM).

properties.name: geoDisjoint
properties.definition: Represents spatial relations in which two geometries (or the places they represent) are topologically disjoint: "they have no point in common. They form a set of disconnected geometries." (A symmetric relationship, as defined in [DE-9IM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DE-9IM).)

properties.name: geoEquals
properties.definition: Represents spatial relations in which two geometries (or the places they represent) are topologically equal, as defined in [DE-9IM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DE-9IM). "Two geometries are topologically equal if their interiors intersect and no part of the interior or boundary of one geometry intersects the exterior of the other" (a symmetric relationship).

properties.name: geoIntersects
properties.definition: Represents spatial relations in which two geometries (or the places they represent) have at least one point in common. As defined in [DE-9IM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DE-9IM).

properties.name: geoOverlaps
properties.definition: Represents a relationship between two geometries (or the places they represent), relating a geometry to another that geospatially overlaps it, i.e. they have some but not all points in common. As defined in [DE-9IM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DE-9IM).

properties.name: geoTouches
properties.definition: Represents spatial relations in which two geometries (or the places they represent) touch: "they have at least one boundary point in common, but no interior points." (A symmetric relationship, as defined in [DE-9IM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DE-9IM).)

properties.name: geoWithin
properties.definition: Represents a relationship between two geometries (or the places they represent), relating a geometry to one that contains it, i.e. it is inside (i.e. within) its interior. As defined in [DE-9IM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DE-9IM).
