properties.definition: Review of the item.

properties.name: slogan
properties.definition: A slogan or motto associated with the item.

properties.name: smokingAllowed
properties.definition: Indicates whether it is allowed to smoke in the place, e.g. in the restaurant, hotel or hotel room.

properties.name: specialOpeningHoursSpecification
properties.definition: The special opening hours of a certain place.\n\nUse this to explicitly override general opening hours brought in scope by [[openingHoursSpecification]] or [[openingHours]].

properties.name: telephone
properties.definition: The telephone number.

properties.name: tourBookingPage
properties.definition: A page providing information on how to book a tour of some [[Place]], such as an [[Accommodation]] or [[ApartmentComplex]] in a real estate setting, as well as other kinds of tours as appropriate.

classes.name: Protein
classes.definition: Protein is here used in its widest possible definition, as classes of amino acid based molecules. Amyloid-beta Protein in human (UniProt P05067), eukaryota (e.g. an OrthoDB group) or even a single molecule that one can point to are all of type :Protein. A protein can thus be a subclass of another protein, e.g. :Protein as a UniProt record can have multiple isoforms inside it which would also be :Protein. They can be imagined, synthetic, hypothetical or naturally occurring.

properties.name: hasBioPolymerSequence
properties.definition: A symbolic representation of a BioChemEntity. For example, a nucleotide sequence of a Gene or an amino acid sequence of a Protein.

classes.name: ScholarlyArticle
classes.definition: A scholarly article.

classes.name: SoftwareApplication
classes.definition: A software application.

properties.name: applicationCategory
properties.definition: Type of software application, e.g. 'Game, Multimedia'.

properties.name: applicationSubCategory
properties.definition: Subcategory of the application, e.g. 'Arcade Game'.

properties.name: applicationSuite
properties.definition: The name of the application suite to which the application belongs (e.g. Excel belongs to Office).

properties.name: availableOnDevice
properties.definition: Device required to run the application. Used in cases where a specific make/model is required to run the application.

properties.name: countriesNotSupported
properties.definition: Countries for which the application is not supported. You can also provide the two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.

properties.name: countriesSupported
properties.definition: Countries for which the application is supported. You can also provide the two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.

properties.name: device
properties.definition: Device required to run the application. Used in cases where a specific make/model is required to run the application.

properties.name: downloadUrl
properties.definition: If the file can be downloaded, URL to download the binary.

properties.name: featureList
properties.definition: Features or modules provided by this application (and possibly required by other applications).

properties.name: fileSize
properties.definition: Size of the application / package (e.g. 18MB). In the absence of a unit (MB, KB etc.), KB will be assumed.

properties.name: installUrl
properties.definition: URL at which the app may be installed, if different from the URL of the item.

properties.name: memoryRequirements
properties.definition: Minimum memory requirements.

properties.name: operatingSystem
properties.definition: Operating systems supported (Windows 7, OS X 10.6, Android 1.6).

properties.name: permissions
properties.definition: Permission(s) required to run the app (for example, a mobile app may require full internet access or may run only on wifi).

properties.name: processorRequirements
properties.definition: Processor architecture required to run the application (e.g. IA64).

properties.name: releaseNotes
properties.definition: Description of what changed in this version.

properties.name: requirements
properties.definition: Component dependency requirements for application. This includes runtime environments and shared libraries that are not included in the application distribution package, but required to run the application (examples: DirectX, Java or .NET runtime).

properties.name: runtimePlatform
properties.definition: Runtime platform or script interpreter dependencies (example: Java v1, Python 2.3, .NET Framework 3.0).

properties.name: screenshot
properties.definition: A link to a screenshot image of the app.

properties.name: softwareAddOn
properties.definition: Additional content for a software application.

properties.name: softwareHelp
properties.definition: Software application help.

properties.name: softwareRequirements
properties.definition: Component dependency requirements for application. This includes runtime environments and shared libraries that are not included in the application distribution package, but required to run the application (examples: DirectX, Java or .NET runtime).

properties.name: softwareVersion
properties.definition: Version of the software instance.

properties.name: storageRequirements
properties.definition: Storage requirements (free space required).

properties.name: supportingData
properties.definition: Supporting data for a SoftwareApplication.

classes.name: SoftwareSourceCode
classes.definition: Computer programming source code. Example: Full (compile ready) solutions, code snippet samples, scripts, templates.

properties.name: codeRepository
properties.definition: Link to the repository where the un-compiled, human readable code and related code is located (SVN, GitHub, CodePlex).

properties.name: codeSampleType
properties.definition: What type of code sample: full (compile ready) solution, code snippet, inline code, scripts, template.

properties.name: programmingLanguage
properties.definition: The computer programming language.

properties.name: runtime
properties.definition: Runtime platform or script interpreter dependencies (example: Java v1, Python 2.3, .NET Framework 3.0).

properties.name: runtimePlatform
properties.definition: Runtime platform or script interpreter dependencies (example: Java v1, Python 2.3, .NET Framework 3.0).

properties.name: sampleType
properties.definition: What type of code sample: full (compile ready) solution, code snippet, inline code, scripts, template.

properties.name: targetProduct
properties.definition: Target Operating System / Product to which the code applies.  If applies to several versions, just the product name can be used.

classes.name: Substance
classes.definition: Any matter of defined composition that has discrete existence, whose origin may be biological, mineral or chemical.

properties.name: activeIngredient
properties.definition: An active ingredient, typically chemical compounds and/or biologic substances.

properties.name: maximumIntake
properties.definition: Recommended intake of this supplement for a given population as defined by a specific recommending authority.

classes.name: TherapeuticProcedure
classes.definition: A medical procedure intended primarily for therapeutic purposes, aimed at improving a health condition.

properties.name: adverseOutcome
properties.definition: A possible complication and/or side effect of this therapy. If it is known that an adverse outcome is serious (resulting in death, disability, or permanent damage; requiring hospitalization; or otherwise life-threatening or requiring immediate medical attention), tag it as a seriousAdverseOutcome instead.

properties.name: doseSchedule
properties.definition: A dosing schedule for the drug for a given population, either observed, recommended, or maximum dose based on the type used.

properties.name: drug
properties.definition: Specifying a drug or medicine used in a medication procedure.

classes.name: Thing
classes.definition: The most generic type of item.

properties.name: additionalType
properties.definition: An additional type for the item, typically used for adding more specific types from external vocabularies in microdata syntax. This is a relationship between something and a class that the thing is in. Typically the value is a URI-identified RDF class, and in this case corresponds to the use of rdf:type in RDF. Text values can be used sparingly, for cases where useful information can be added without their being an appropriate schema to reference. In the case of text values, the class label should follow the schema.org style guide.

properties.name: alternateName
properties.definition: An alias for the item.

properties.name: description
properties.definition: A description of the item.

properties.name: disambiguatingDescription
properties.definition: A sub property of description. A short description of the item used to disambiguate from other, similar items. Information from other properties (in particular, name) may be necessary for the description to be useful for disambiguation.

properties.name: identifier
properties.definition: The identifier property represents any kind of identifier for any kind of [[Thing]], such as ISBNs, GTIN codes, UUIDs etc. Schema.org provides dedicated properties for representing many of these, either as textual strings or as URL (URI) links. See [background notes](/docs/datamodel.html#identifierBg) for more details.

properties.name: image
properties.definition: An image of the item. This can be a [[URL]] or a fully described [[ImageObject]].

properties.name: mainEntityOfPage
properties.definition: Indicates a page (or other CreativeWork) for which this thing is the main entity being described. See [background notes](/docs/datamodel.html#mainEntityBackground) for details.

properties.name: name
properties.definition: The name of the item.

properties.name: owner
properties.definition: A person or organization who owns this Thing.

properties.name: potentialAction
properties.definition: Indicates a potential Action, which describes an idealized action in which this thing would play an 'object' role.

properties.name: sameAs
properties.definition: URL of a reference Web page that unambiguously indicates the item's identity. E.g. the URL of the item's Wikipedia page, Wikidata entry, or official website.

properties.name: subjectOf
properties.definition: A CreativeWork or Event about this Thing.

properties.name: url
properties.definition: URL of the item.
